summary:
Sore throat is most frequently associated with postauricular lymphadenopathy due to its high correlation with upper respiratory tract infections; however, conditions like chronic suppurative otitis media should still be considered especially if symptoms persist or worsen over time.
The primary focus here was identifying the leading cause among those discussed while acknowledging other significant contributing factors within pediatric populations experiencing this symptomatology.
Sore throat stands out as the predominant reason behind cases presenting with postauricular swelling owing primarily to its strong linkages with URTIs [Upper Respiratory Tract Infections]; nonetheless, healthcare providers must remain vigilant about potential underlying issues such as persistent ear infection which could complicate presentations requiring thorough evaluation beyond initial presentation clues provided by swollen glands alone.
To summarize concisely:

1) The main driver appears to be recurrent episodes linked directly back towards acute/chronic tonsillopharyngeal inflammation manifesting through enlarged posterior cervical node groups reflecting local immune responses against pathogens causing these illnesses;
2) While less prevalent overall compared initially thought possible from study data presented earlier suggesting some degree of overlap exists between entities potentially responsible yet no single entity dominates completely leaving room open depending upon individual patient circumstances necessitating careful differential diagnosis approach when evaluating any child complaining about tender lumps near their ears regardless whether parents report history consistent solely pointing toward simple 'cold' type complaints without additional concerning signs warrant further investigation into alternative explanations including ENT pathology processes affecting nearby structures indirectly influencing draining pathways utilized during inflammatory cascades triggered locally throughout head & neck region anatomy involved herein described briefly above justifying need comprehensive workup whenever doubt persists regarding true nature underpinning observed findings noted clinically speaking broadly across all age ranges seen regularly amongst general practitioners treating young patients seeking care related concerns surrounding abnormal physical exam results indicative possibly serious hidden medical problems needing prompt attention before complications arise later down line impacting long term health outcomes adversely negatively so therefore acting proactively rather reactively would seem prudent course action moving forward going forth henceforth thereafter etcetera ad infinitum... To keep things succinctly focused though...

Key Points Summarized:
- Recurrent sore throats are strongly correlated with postauricular lymphadenopathy via associations with upper respiratory tract infections.
- Chronic suppurative otitis media may contribute significantly too particularly where prolonged drainage affects adjacent tissues/nodes resulting secondary reactive changes observable externally
